Polar ECLIPSE School

Early Career Learning and Integration in Polar Science Engagement

Polar ECLIPSE School
Schedule Apply About us

Polar ECLIPSE School

[DISCLAIMER: This Website is currently under construction. Information is preliminary. More details and formatting will follow soon.]

Who?

@PolarCommunity

@Scientists, @Artists, @Philosophers, @Mountaineers, @ScienceSupporters, @CommunityBuilders, @KnowledgeHolders, @Guardians

When?

15th - 22nd of August 2026

Where?

Meeting at the boundaries of our worlds: Skagafjörður, Iceland.

What?

We want to strengthen, inspire, and engage the young members of the Polar community.

The total solar eclipse gives space to learn, think, and reflect. This is a philosophical, artistic, outdoor, and natural science workshop. Participants can connect with nature and, through nature, with each other. The workshop aims to strengthen relationships and collaboration among the young (“early-career”) polar community across borders. When datasets, funding, jobs, and subsequently a future are disappearing for a large part of the Polar community, it is time to strengthen our bonds and build resilience.

We encourage especially people with non-traditional careers/education to apply, as well as individuals who are underrepresented in the Polar community.

We commit to EDI principles. Names of participants will not be disclosed without their explicit consent.

Website based on ml4cryo.github.io and criticialscience.github.io. The code relies on jekyll and bulma.

The lunar eclipse featured on this website is by Dan Zafra and used with his kind permission.
